what_it_does: A content-generation engine (MS_PRODUCE) that fills resolved artifact skeletons with real generated content. It composes a prompt from the artifact kind's schema, resolved values, project brief, and memory, asks window.claude.complete for a small JSON object, parses it, and stores the result on the artifact. It also provides refine, approve/reject, and create helpers.
observations: Defines a declarative SCHEMAS map from kind id to field hints, plus CATEGORY_FALLBACK and a generic fallback.
schemaFor resolves the right schema by kind.id, then kind.category, then a generic title+summary.
fieldList turns a schema into a JSON-field description string for the prompt.
prompt() pulls briefLine from MS_VI_BRAIN, project title from MS_CONTEXT or ctx, and recalls up to 4 memory items above score 0.12 from MS_MEMORY.
generate() is async, sets contentStatus to 'generating', calls window.claude.complete, parses the reply, and sets content to 'ready' or 'error'.
parseJSON strips markdown fences and extracts the first '{' to last '}' before JSON.parse.
refine() regenerates content with an extra instruction and stashes the previous content in contentPrev.
decide() marks the artifact approved/rejected, writes an anchor or anti-pattern memory, and calls MS_RESOLVE.noteVariant to influence future generations.
create() creates an artifact via MS_ARTIFACTS.create and immediately triggers content generation unless withContent is false.
Dispatches a CustomEvent 'vi-artifact-content' after successful generation.
standouts: Declarative SCHEMAS map makes adding a new kind's content shape data rather than code.
Prompt is conditioned on the same brain context (brief + memory) used elsewhere, aiming for project-consistent copy.
decide() closes a feedback loop: approval/rejection is remembered and nudges the next generation of the same kind via noteVariant.
conventions: IIFE attached to window as window.MS_PRODUCE.
Defensive optional chaining via existence checks for window.claude, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_MEMORY, MS_RESOLVE, MS_CONTEXT, MS_TOAST.
Async/await with try/catch and explicit status transitions.
Status machine: contentStatus moves through generating -> ready/error.
concerns: parseJSON slices from first '{' to last '}', which can include trailing prose or corrupt nested JSON.
No schema validation of returned fields beyond JSON parse success.
Hardcoded memory recall threshold (0.12) and top-k (4).
No retry, backoff, or fallback model if window.claude.complete fails or is unreachable.
decide() calls A.setStatus rather than a content-specific status; unclear if this changes artifact lifecycle status or content status.
Refinement prompt mentions a previous version but does not actually include the previous content in the prompt.
notes: This is the LLM-backed flesh layer: it maps resolved design skeletons to per-kind JSON content, records feedback, and exposes create/refine/decide helpers.
questions: What exact object does window.claude.complete return, and does it always expose a string reply?
How are SCHEMAS kept in sync with the kind definitions in MS_KINDS?
What consumes contentPrev, and how is the diff presented?
How does MS_RESOLVE.noteVariant actually shift future resolved values?
Is there a content size budget or token limit enforced per kind?
Should the refinement prompt include the previous content JSON for grounded edits?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is described as the content-generation engine that gives flesh to resolved skeletons, and the code does exactly that while also adding a feedback loop.
apparent_themes: Context-aware generation using project brief and memory
Tight feedback loop from approval/rejection to future output
Single-source content engine for all artifact kinds
intention_signals: It is trying to centralize all produced content generation so every artifact kind gets real, project-consistent copy from the same brain context, and to make human approval/rejection shape future outputs.
novelty: A per-kind JSON content schema map combined with memory/brief-conditioned prompting and integrated approval/reject feedback that nudges subsequent variant resolution.
contribution: A schema-driven, context-conditioned content generator with lifecycle helpers (generate, refine, decide, create) and a feedback-to-memory mechanism that can be fused into a larger artifact pipeline.
summary_for_embedding: Schema-driven JavaScript module that generates per-kind JSON content for design artifacts via an LLM, using project brief, resolved values, and memory, with refine, approval/reject feedback, and artifact creation helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, declarative content-generation engine that fills resolved artifact skeletons with project-consistent copy and learns from approval/rejection feedback.

symbols.does: Selects the content schema for a kind, falling back by category and then a generic shape.
symbols.description: schemaFor looks up the kind's id in SCHEMAS, then its category in CATEGORY_FALLBACK, and finally returns a generic title+summary schema. It is the single place that decides what fields the model must produce.

symbols.does: Renders a schema as a JSON-field description block for the prompt.
symbols.description: fieldList walks the schema entries and emits lines like '"field": "hint"' or '"field": [hint]', which are inserted into the prompt so the model knows the exact expected output shape.

symbols.does: Composes the generation prompt from kind, resolved values, project brief, and memory.
symbols.description: prompt builds a user message that identifies Vi and the artifact kind, includes the project aim from MS_VI_BRAIN, relevant memory from MS_MEMORY, the resolved values, and the expected JSON fields. It is the context-conditioning step for content generation.

symbols.does: Strips markdown fences and extracts a JSON object from the model reply.
symbols.description: parseJSON trims the reply, removes leading ```/```json fences, finds the first '{' and last '}', and attempts JSON.parse. It returns null on any failure, which triggers the error status path.

symbols.does: Generates and stores content for an artifact via the LLM.
symbols.description: generate resolves the artifact, sets contentStatus to 'generating', calls window.claude.complete with the composed prompt, parses the JSON, stores it via MS_ARTIFACTS.setContent, sets status to 'ready', and dispatches a vi-artifact-content event. On failure it logs and marks 'error'.

symbols.does: Regenerates content with an instruction while preserving the previous version.
symbols.description: refine calls generate with keepPrev=true and an instruction appended to the prompt, allowing the UI to diff the old and new content while steering the model to revise specific aspects.

symbols.does: Records approval or rejection and feeds it into memory and future variant resolution.
symbols.description: decide sets the artifact status to approved or rejected, writes a weighted memory entry to MS_MEMORY, and calls MS_RESOLVE.noteVariant so the next generation of that kind leans toward or away from the current variant. It also shows a toast.

symbols.does: Creates an artifact and immediately starts content generation.
symbols.description: create uses MS_ARTIFACTS.create to instantiate an artifact for a kind, then calls generate on it unless withContent is false. It returns the artifact immediately while content fills in asynchronously.
